intro: Neopringlea viscosa, also known as the sticky Pringlea, is a flowering plant native to the island of Kerguelen in the southern Indian Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neopringlea viscosa include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neopringlea viscosa is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to make a cough syrup or expectorant, capitalizing on its expectorant properties and soothing effects on the respiratory system.
